original: Roman scrolls burnt in Mt Vesuvius eruption at Herculaneum read for the first time, after computer-science student develops machine-learning algorithm - first word deciphered is purple
Hirokazu Yasuhara, Japanese video game designer (Sonic the Hedgehog), born in Japan
Typhoon Tip becomes the most intense tropical cyclone ever recorded with peak sustained winds of 305km/h (190mph) and a wind diameter of 2,220km (1,380mi)
Auston Matthews becomes 1st player to score 4 goals in his NHL debut for the Toronto Maple Leafs at Ottawa
Eliud Kipchogeof Kenya becomes first to run a marathon in under 2 hours (1:59:40) in Vienna, Austria; not a race by strict definition using pacesetters and pace laser markings on a doctored course
Christ the Redeemer statue opens standing 30 meters high (98 ft) on top of Mount Corcovado overlooking Rio de Janeiro, built by engineer Heitor da Silva Costa
Mariner 9 takes pictures of Martian north pole
Soviet leaderNikita Khrushchevbangs his shoe on his desk at UN General Assembly session
King Johnof England loses his crown jewels in The Wash as the flood tide swamps his wagons, probably near Fosdyke, perhaps near Sutton Bridge
The second Chinese human spaceflight Shenzhou 6 launched carrying Fèi Jùnlóng and Niè Hǎishèng for five days in orbit.
Arecibo radio telescope begins Microwave Observing Project search for occupied planets
DreamWorks SKG film studio is founded by director and producerSteven Spielberg, former Disney executive Jeffrey Katzenberg, and music executiveDavid Geffen
Children's rhyme "Three Blind Mice" is published in London in a book edited by and possibly written by Thomas Ravenscroft
1st time 5 people in space simultaneously (Soyez 6 & Soyez 7)
Launch of Voskhod 1, first time 3 people in space (Komarov, Feokistov, Yegorov)
The Day of Six Billion is proclaimed as the 6 billionth living human in the world is born
Doris Miller, American US Navy cook and Pearl Harbor hero (Navy Cross), born in Waco, Texas (d. 1943)[1]
Man O'War's last race & win
First modern submarine is commissioned by the US Navy as the USS Holland, named for its designerJohn Philip Holland
Ford Motor Company underHenry Fordmanufactures its 1 millionth automobile at the River Rouge plant in Detroit
Enriqueta Basilio, Mexican track and field athlete becomes 1st woman to light Olympic flame at 19th Summer Olympics, in Mexico City, Mexico
IXX Summer Olympic Games open at Mexico City, Mexico; first Olympics in Latin America
Long-lost bust of Napoleon byAuguste Rodinconfirmed found in Madison borough hall, New Jersey, worth at least $4m
John Dillingerescapes from jail in Allen County, Ohio
Mike Lerch of Princeton ties NCAA record with 370 receiving yards
1st use of iron lung (Boston's Children Hospital)
4th test of space shuttle Enterprise
NASA launches space vehicle S-195
China announces it will test entire city of Qingdao, 9 million people, for COVID-19 within five days after a dozen cases discovered
Typhoon Hagibis makes landfall on the Izu Peninsula near Tokyo, Japan, bringing record rainfall and killing at least 56 people
The lowest recorded non-tornadic atmospheric pressure, 87.0 kPa (870 mbar or 25.69 inHg), occurred in Guam, in the Western Pacific during Typhoon Tip
American Jacqueline Hansen runs women's world record marathon 2:38:19 in the Nike OTC Marathon, Eugene, Oregon
Boston Celtics guard Chris Ford scores first 3-point basket in NBA history in 1st quarter of 114-106 win v Houston at Boston Garden; game also marks debut of Boston rookieLarry Bird
Future Basketball Hall of Fame forwardMagic Johnsonmakes his debut for Los Angeles Lakers at the San Diego Clippers; Lakers win, 103-102
FIFA Women's World Cup Final, Home Depot Center, Carson, CA: Nia Künzer scores winner in sudden death extra time as Germany beats Sweden, 2-1
Satoshi Kon, Japanese anime film director (Perfect Blue, Paprika), born in Sapporo, Hokkaido, Japan (d. 2010)
Charlie Ward, American basketball guard (NBA: NY Knicks) and College Football Hall of Fame quarterback (Heisman Trophy 1993, Florida State), born in Tallahassee, Florida
American Don Schollander swims an Olympic record 53.4s to beat Briton Robert McGregor by 0.1s and win the 100m freestyle gold medal in Tokyo; first of Schollander's 4 gold medals at the Games
Soviet spacecraft Soyuz 7 is launched from Baikonur Cosmodrome in southern Kazakhstan
Infamous Columbus Day Storm strikes the U.S. Pacific Northwest with record wind velocities; 46 dead and at least U.S. $230 million in damages
Centre-backSergio Ramosbecomes Europe's most capped male outfield soccer player (168 caps) as Spain draws 1-1 with Norway in Euro 2020 qualification in Oslo
German Ferrari driverMichael Schumacherfinishes 8th at the season-ending Japanese F1 Grand Prix at Suzuka; clinches 4th consecutive World Drivers Championship by 2 points fromKimi Räikkönen
Timothy Bradley defeats Juan Manuel Márquez to win the WBA Welterweight boxing title
Martie Maguire, American country fiddler, songwriter, and vocalist (The Chicks, formerly known as Dixie Chicks; Court Yard Hounds), born in York, Pennsylvania
Alison Peek, Australian field hockey halfback and midfielder (Olympics gold 2000), born in Adelaide, South Australia
Melvin Franklin (né English), American R&B bass vocalist (The Temptations, 1960-94 - "Ball of Confusion"), born in Montgomery, Alabama (d. 1995)
Sam Moore, American R&B and soul vocalist (Sam & Dave - "Soul Man", "I Thank You"), born in Miami, Florida (d. 2025)[1]
James "Sugar Boy" Crawford, American R&B singer, pianist, and songwriter ("Jock-A-Mo" aka "Iko Iko"), born in New Orleans, Louisiana (d. 2012)
Charles Macintosh of Scotland begins selling raincoats (Macs)
Self-proclaimed Emperor of the USA,Emperor Nortonissues edict abolishing the US Congress
"Columbus Day Riot": 30,000 fans descend on NYC's Times Square for singerFrank Sinatra's performance at Paramount Theatre (capacity 3,500)
Paramount Pictures and Motown Productions release film "Lady Sings the Blues", very loosely based on singerBillie Holiday's autobiography, starringDiana RossandBilly Dee Williams
George Harrisonreleases single "Got My Mind Set On You", it becomes his third solo #1 hit, and the last chart-topper by any of the former Beatles
Jukebox musical "Buddy: TheBuddy HollyStory" with Paul Hipp, opens at the Victoria Palace Theatre in London's West End
WSBK TV channel 38 in Boston, MA (IND/ABC/CBS/NBC) begin
US/USSR sign joint space effort in telecommunications & meteorology
Contact with NASA Venus orbitter Magellan broken
Challenger moves to Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center (Florida) for maintenance of STS 61A mission
Oscar Arias (Costa Rican Pres) wins Nobel Peace Prize
Former US Vice PresidentAl Goreis awarded the Nobel Peace Prize (jointly with the IPCC)
